# Wasm Knowledge Base Log

Append new entries; do not rewrite prior history except to fix obvious formatting mistakes or redact sensitive data.

## [2026-06-25] passes/optimize-instructions | Cover selected-eleventh multi-result tuple boundary

- Added OI-M direct-HOT boundary coverage for selecting the eleventh scalar lane from a multi-result tuple child.
- Binaryen `version_130` localizes the selected eleventh result through tuple scratch plus scalar temps; Starshine intentionally keeps the direct-HOT tuple spelling until selected-child tuple-scratch localization exists.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-m-tuple-multiresult-selected-eleventh-probe.wat`; focused `moon test --target native src/passes/optimize_instructions_test.mbt --filter '*selected eleventh lane*'` passed `1/1`.

## [2026-06-25] passes/optimize-instructions | Cover size-13 bulk-memory boundary

- Added OI-G public-pipeline boundary coverage for size-13 `memory.copy` and `memory.fill`.

## [2026-06-25] passes/optimize-instructions | Carry wrap maxBits facts

- Added OI-D red-first coverage for `i32.wrap_i64` over a bounded i64 producer feeding direct and local-carried maxBits comparisons.
- Starshine now inherits an i32 unsigned max fact from `i32.wrap_i64` only when the wrapped i64 producer has a proven nonnegative maximum no larger than `i32::MAX`, enabling direct out-of-range folds and local-carried signed-to-unsigned spelling rewrites.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-wrap-maxbits-probe.wat`; focused `moon test --target native src/passes/optimize_instructions_test.mbt --filter '*i32.wrap_i64 unsigned maxBits*'` failed before implementation and passed `1/1` after.

## [2026-06-25] passes/optimize-instructions | Cover selected trapping tuple lane with sibling

- Added OI-M direct-HOT coverage proving one-use `tuple.extract(tuple.make(...))` localization preserves a selected trapping `i32.load` before a later effectful sibling call.
- Binaryen `version_130` preserves the selected load and sibling call through tuple scratch; Starshine's direct-HOT localizer preserves the same order by storing the exact selected load, dropping the sibling call, and reloading the selected temp.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Admit load-call constant offsets through OI raw gate

- Narrowed `load-call-optimize-instructions-noop` for the exact `i32.const; nonzero-offset scalar load; drop; call` shape, allowing direct OI's constant-pointer static-offset fold to run even when a direct call follows the dropped load.
- Starshine now rewrites the public-pipeline fixture `i32.const 8; i32.load offset=4; drop; call` to use `i32.const 12` with zero load offset, matching Binaryen `version_130` for the source-backed case.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-g-load-call-offset-next-probe.wat`; red-first `*load-call constant memory offsets*` failed before implementation with `load-call-optimize-instructions-noop` and passed after. Broader mixed load/call functions remain behind the raw gate.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Fold same-local integer binaries

- Added direct OI-D same-local integer binary folding: identical direct `local.get` operands under `sub` and `xor` become zero, while identical direct `local.get` operands under `and` and `or` become the local value, for i32 and i64.
- Kept the proof intentionally narrow to direct locals only; broader expression identity, effectful operands, and flow-sensitive equality facts remain open.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-same-local-binary-probe.wat`; red-first `*same-local integer binary operands*` failed before implementation and passed after.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Lower effectful tiny memory.copy

- Narrowed the OI raw `stack-carried-effect` gate for flat tiny `memory.copy` sequences whose destination/source operands are no-param one-result direct calls or pure local/constant values and whose size is `1`/`2`/`4`/`8`.
- The existing HOT tiny-copy lowering now rewrites those public-pipeline shapes to load/store pairs while preserving destination-before-source operand evaluation order, matching Binaryen-observed size-1 and size-8 call-backed copies.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-g-effectful-memory-copy-next-probe.wat`; red-first `*effectful tiny memory.copy*` failed before implementation with `stack-carried-effect-optimize-instructions-noop` and passed after. Non-flat, parameterized-call, nonconstant-size, zero-size, and broader control/effect memory-copy localization remains open.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Rewrite reinterpret stores

- Added the OI-G direct stored-value cleanup for reinterpret-store pairs: `f32.store(f32.reinterpret_i32 x)` becomes `i32.store x`, `f64.store(f64.reinterpret_i64 x)` becomes `i64.store x`, and the integer-store inverse forms become `f32.store` / `f64.store`.
- The rewrite preserves the original address and memarg while storing the same bit representation through the source value's representation type.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-g-reinterpret-store-probe.wat`; red-first `*reinterpret stores*` failed before implementation and passed after. Broader stored-value canonicalization, effectful memory-copy localization, and load/call raw-gate escapes remain open.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Recurse direct maxBits compare facts

- Extended the OI-D `maxBits`-style unsigned compare helper so direct `and` and constant `shr_u` facts can feed each other recursively for i32 and i64.
- Starshine now folds nested shapes such as `((x & 1023) >>> 4) == 64` to `i32.const 0`, and preserves effectful nested shifted operands as `drop(i32.shr_u(i32.and(call, 1023), 4)); i32.const 0`, matching the observed Binaryen `version_130` shape.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-recursive-maxbits-probe.wat`; red-first `*recursive unsigned maxBits compares*` failed before implementation and passed after. Dynamic/zero shifts, signed range proofs, local-scanner facts, select/phi/load/extension facts, and broader recursive expressions remain open.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Fold i64 unsigned-shift bounded compares

- Extended the OI-D `maxBits`-style unsigned shift compare fold to i64: direct `i64.shr_u(x, const)` with shift amount `1..63` bounds the result below the compared out-of-range constant.
- Pure shifted operands fold directly to `i32.const`; effectful/trapping shifted operands are preserved with `drop(i64.shr_u(...)); i32.const result`, matching Binaryen's observed `version_130` shape for a call-backed shift.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i64-shr-maxbits-probe.wat`; red-first `*i64 unsigned-shift bounded compares*` failed before implementation and passed after. Dynamic/zero shift amounts, signed range facts, and broader recursive `maxBits` lattice proofs remain open.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Fold i32 unsigned-shift bounded compares

- Extended the OI-D `maxBits`-style unsigned compare fold beyond direct `and` masks for a first recursive scalar fact: direct `i32.shr_u(x, const)` with shift amount `1..31` bounds the result below the compared out-of-range constant.
- Pure shifted operands fold directly to `i32.const`; effectful/trapping shifted operands are preserved with `drop(i32.shr_u(...)); i32.const result`, matching Binaryen's observed `version_130` shape for a call-backed shift.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-shr-maxbits-probe.wat`; red-first `*i32 unsigned-shift bounded compares*` failed before implementation and passed after. The proof remains narrow: no shift-by-zero/full-u32 proof, dynamic shift amount, signed range, or broader recursive `maxBits` lattice is claimed.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Preserve i64 masked unsigned compare effects

- Extended the OI-D `maxBits`-style i64 masked unsigned compare fold to preserve effectful/trapping direct masked operands: when the compared constant is outside the nonnegative `i64.and` mask range, Starshine now replaces the compare with `drop(masked-value); i32.const result` instead of skipping the fold.
- This closes the effect-preserving counterpart to the prior i32 direct-mask slice, but keeps recursive maxBits facts beyond direct nonnegative masks, signed range proofs, and non-mask width facts open.
- Evidence: Binaryen `version_130` oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i64-mask-compare-effects-probe.wat` folds the call-backed i64 mask compare to `drop(i64.and(call, 255)); i32.const 0`; red-first `*i64 masked unsigned compare effects*` failed before implementation and passed after.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Preserve i32 masked unsigned compare effects

- Extended the OI-D `maxBits`-style i32 masked unsigned compare fold to preserve effectful/trapping direct masked operands: when the compared constant is outside the nonnegative `i32.and` mask range, Starshine now replaces the compare with `drop(masked-value); i32.const result` instead of skipping the fold.
- Kept the slice narrow: it handles direct i32 `and` masks only; effect-preserving i64, recursive maxBits facts beyond direct nonnegative masks, signed range proofs, and non-mask width facts remain open.
- Evidence: Binaryen `version_130` oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-mask-compare-probe.wat` folds the call-backed i32 mask compare to `drop(i32.and(call, 255)); i32.const 0`; red-first `*i32 masked unsigned compare effects*` failed before implementation and passed after.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Fold pure i64 masked unsigned compares

- Extended the OI-D `maxBits`-style masked unsigned compare slice to i64: a side-effect-free direct `i64.and` with a nonnegative constant mask can now fold `eq`/`ne` and unsigned relational compares when the compared constant is outside the masked range.
- Kept the same narrow boundary as the i32 slice: effectful masked operands, recursive maxBits facts beyond direct nonnegative `and` masks, signed range proofs, and non-mask width facts remain open.
- Evidence: Binaryen `version_130` oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i64-mask-compare-probe.wat`; red-first `*i64 masked unsigned compares*` failed before implementation and passed after.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Fold pure i32 masked unsigned compares

- Added a first OI-D `maxBits`-style scalar compare slice for pure i32 `and` masks: when a nonnegative constant mask bounds the left operand below an out-of-range constant, Starshine folds `eq`/`ne` and unsigned relational compares to `i32.const 0` or `i32.const 1`.
- Kept the proof deliberately narrow: it requires a side-effect-free direct `i32.and` mask and a constant RHS; effectful masked operands, recursive maxBits facts, signed range proofs, and i64 masked values remain open for separate slices.
- Evidence: Binaryen `version_130` oracle probes `.tmp/oi-d-mask-compare-probe.wat` and `.tmp/oi-d-mask-compare-probe2.wat`; red-first `*i32 masked unsigned compares*` failed before implementation and passed after.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Fold same-local integer compares

- Added an OI-D scalar compare slice for identical integer local operands: `eq`/`le`/`ge` fold to `i32.const 1`, while `ne`/`lt`/`gt` fold to `i32.const 0`, for i32 and i64 signed/unsigned compare opcodes.
- Kept the proof deliberately narrow: the fold requires both operands to be direct `local.get` of the same local, so no effectful, trapping, float/NaN-sensitive, or broader expression identity is claimed.
- Evidence: Binaryen `version_130` oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-same-local-integer-compare-probe.wat` folds the covered shapes to constants; red-first `*same-local integer compares*` failed before implementation and passed after.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Admit simple stack-carried call operands through OI raw gate

- Narrowed `stack-carried-effect-optimize-instructions-noop` for the exact straight-line `pure local.get/const; no-param direct call; commutative integer binop` subset, allowing public `local.get + call` fixtures to reach HOT and use the call-first commutative canonicalizer added earlier.
- Kept broader stack-carried effects fail-closed: load/call mixtures, local writes, memory.copy call-result operands, call operands with parameters, indirect/reference calls, multi-result calls, and control-bearing forms still require separate source-backed localization/gate slices.
- Evidence: red-first focused public-pipeline test `*stack-carried call operands*` failed with `skip-raw reason=stack-carried-effect-optimize-instructions-noop`; after the narrow raw-gate escape it passed and asserted no raw skip plus `call (Func 1)` before `local.get (Local 0)` in the optimized body. Docs/backlog now describe the raw-gate escape as narrow rather than broad call-operand parity.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Add guarded call ranks to commutative canonicalization

- Added `HotOp::Call`, `HotOp::CallIndirect`, and `HotOp::CallRef` to the optimize-instructions commutative kind-rank table ahead of locals/constants, matching Binaryen's call-first commutative spelling for safe shapes such as `local.get + call` and `const * call`.
- Kept call reordering guarded by `optimize_instructions_subtrees_can_swap`: `load + call` remains in source order because the call may write memory observed by the load, and `local.tee + call` remains in source order because the call may trap after a side-effecting tee. Post-mul-negation `local * call` factors now canonicalize to `call * local` when the negation rewrite strips wrappers in place.
- Updated `src/passes/optimize_instructions.mbt`, `src/passes/optimize_instructions_test.mbt`, `agent-todo.md`, and the optimize-instructions wiki strategy/WAT-shape pages. Evidence: red-first `*commutative call operands*` failed before the rank change and passed after implementation, including direct HOT coverage for `call_indirect` and `call_ref` ranks; a local Binaryen `version_130` WAT probe in `.tmp/oi-call-commutative-probe.wat` showed call-first safe shapes and unchanged load/call conflict shapes. A public Starshine replay of that binary fixture still hit the existing `stack-carried-effect-optimize-instructions-noop` raw gate for stack-style call-operand functions, so this slice closes the HOT canonicalizer rank gap but not the raw-gate escape/signoff problem.

## [2026-06-24] passes/optimize-instructions | Re-enable guarded commutative canonicalization and document reorder contracts

- Made the leading-rewrite soundness contract precise across the optimize-instructions wiki: the leading `(0 - x) + y -> y - x` rewrite reorders the two value computations and is gated by `optimize_instructions_subtrees_can_swap` (no RAW/WAR/WAW region conflict on locals/globals/memory/tables; no may-trap/throw operand past a side effect; no control-flow operands), mirroring Binaryen's reorder rule. The trailing `y + (0 - x) -> y - x` needs no guard. The sibling `-x * -y -> x * y` strips both `(0 - x)` wrappers in place, so it needs no reorder proof and applies even for effectful factors such as `(0 - call) * (0 - y)`.
- Re-enabled `optimize_instructions_try_canonicalize_commutative` for ranked non-call HOT value nodes using the same `subtrees_can_swap` proof, restoring safe canonicalization for leading constants, local-get ordering, and nested expression/local ordering while keeping same-region local/memory conflicts, may-trap past side-effect hazards, and control-flow operands blocked. `HotOp::Call`/`CallIndirect`/`CallRef` still carry no kind rank, so general call-operand commutative ordering remains a follow-up slice.
- Updated `src/passes/optimize_instructions.mbt`, `src/passes/optimize_instructions_test.mbt`, `binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/{wat-shapes,binaryen-strategy,starshine-strategy,starshine-hot-ir-strategy}.md`, and `agent-todo.md`. Evidence: red-first `*canonicalizes leading commutative constants*` failed before implementation; focused `*commutative*`, focused `*optimize-instructions*`, `moon test src/passes`, `moon fmt`, `moon info`, `git diff --check`, and native `src/cmd` build passed. A direct 10-case compare smoke after the native build compared `10/10` with `10` raw mismatches; sampled call/local output confirms the intentionally open call-operand canonicalizer rank gap, while broader generated mismatches remain covered by the still-active OI audit rather than closeout evidence. A direct 100-case smoke timed out before `result.json`.

## [2026-06-23] passes/optimize-instructions | Implement OI-D identity binary folds

- Filed [`raw/research/0838-2026-06-23-optimize-instructions-oi-d-identity-binary.md`](raw/research/0838-2026-06-23-optimize-instructions-oi-d-identity-binary.md) extending the completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-D]` default-scalar surface with identity binary-operand folding. Binaryen `version_130` direct `--optimize-instructions` folds `or`/`xor`/`add`/`shl`/`shr_u`/`shr_s` with a `0` operand, `and` with `-1`, `mul` with `1`, and `sub` with a `0` right operand (i32 and i64) to the other operand, and keeps `sub(0, x)`; Starshine now matches that.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle probes `.tmp/oi-g-identity-binary-probe.wat` and `.tmp/oi-g-add-sub-xor-probe.wat` folded every standalone identity and kept `sub(0, x)`. Red-first `*folds identity binary operands*` failed before implementation and passed `1/1` after; `*optimize-instructions*` passed `224/224`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` (`2754/2754`), native `src/cmd` build, `moon info`, and diff checks passed. Direct count-1 compare smoke compared `1/1` with one unrelated raw scalar mismatch and matching covered-binop counts (input 48 -> 33 on both Starshine and Binaryen), confirming identity-fold parity on the case. `div`/`rem`/`rotl`/`rotr` identities, float identities, and broader strength-reduction remain open.

Direct count-1 compare smoke compared `1/1` with one unrelated raw scalar mismatch and no `wrap_i64` / store occurrences in either output artifact. The reverse extend direction, multi-use wraps, f32/f64 and atomic stores, and broader `optimizeStoredValue` / `maxBits` recursive analysis remain open.

Binaryen `version_130` `--optimize-instructions` (direct and O4z-style `-O`) does not fold fresh-array `array.fill` or `array.copy` for any probed pure/effectful in-bounds or out-of-bounds shape; Starshine keeps them too, so this is correct OI parity rather than a gap. No optimizer source changed, so the native build / `moon info` / compare smoke were not rerun. Effectful/localizing and overlap-safe `array.fill`/`array.copy` rewrites stay open only as broader localizing work, not OI parity gaps; the remaining open OI-K items are blocked on safe localizing/HOT lowering or HOT descriptor-operand support, or are out of OI scope (shared/atomic).

Starshine already folds `ref.null struct; ref.test (ref struct)` to `i32.const 0`, rewrites matching non-null `ref.cast` to `unreachable`, and preserves already-evaluated `drop(call $effect)` prefixes before those folded/trapping suffixes.

Focused `*known-null ref.test*` passed immediately (`2/2`); final `*ref.test and ref.cast*` passed `25/25`, `*ref*` passed `62/62`, final `*optimize-instructions*` passed `192/192`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` (`2722/2722`), native `src/cmd` build, `moon info`, and diff checks passed. ## [2026-06-25] passes/code-pushing | Document TNH context blocker

- Filed [`raw/research/0894-2026-06-25-code-pushing-tnh-context-blocker.md`](raw/research/0894-2026-06-25-code-pushing-tnh-context-blocker.md) for `[CP-BINREP-002]` after inspecting the local trap-mode plumbing.
- Finding: `HotPipelineOptions` and CLI config carry `traps_never_happen`, but `HotPassContext` does not expose trap mode to hot function passes, while `code_pushing_node_is_movable_value(...)` currently rejects exact trapping integer div/rem opcodes unconditionally.
- This is a blocker, not an accepted non-goal: `[CP-BINREP-002]` remains open and should add red default-vs-TNH tests plus explicit hot-pass context plumbing/API snapshot review before relaxing code-pushing trap gates.

## [2026-06-25] passes/code-pushing | Keep rethrowing legacy try stationary

- Starshine added a resolved-block HOT builder for this multi-result test shape and widened only the block-label `BrOnNonNull` push-point gate. Loop-label `br_on_*`, broader reference-carrying forms, aggregate GenValid coverage, and multi-set prefix-payload variants remain open.

## [2026-06-25] passes/code-pushing | Refresh value-br_if lowering blocker

- Agent classification: size-losing value-`br_if` lowering/normalization blocker, not a missing `code-pushing` movement proof. Both outputs sink the local sets after the value-branch `br_if`, but Starshine materializes the fallthrough value through an extra temporary local and drops it while Binaryen emits `drop (br_if ...)`. Keep `code-pushing-br-if-value` targeted-only and out of `code-pushing-all` until fixed, narrowly normalized, or explicitly accepted with size/semantic evidence.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Add final-root no-swap coverage

- Filed [`raw/research/0799-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-final-root-no-swap.md`](raw/research/0799-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-final-root-no-swap.md). A local `wasm-opt --all-features --heap-store-optimization -S` probe kept a fresh `struct.new_default` local assignment before a final `i32.const 42` result root, matching the source comment that swapping the final root is useless for finding a later `struct.set` and can disturb stack shape. ## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Add active-catch throw negative coverage

- Filed [`raw/research/0798-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-active-catch-throw-negative.md`](raw/research/0798-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-active-catch-throw-negative.md). Promoted the Binaryen `version_130` active-catch conditional `throw` negative from `0797` into focused HSO coverage. Starshine already matched the oracle after the external-exit fix: conditional `throw` without an active in-function catch folds, `return_call` inside an active catch folds, but conditional `throw` inside a catchable `try_table` keeps `struct.set` because the thrown path can skip the delayed fresh-struct local assignment by transferring to the in-function catch. Focused HSO tests passed `82/82`. No implementation code changed, so no native rebuild or direct compare was required for this coverage-only slice. Broader in-function branch/catch negatives remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix external-exit control folds

- Filed [`raw/research/0797-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-external-exits.md`](raw/research/0797-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-external-exits.md). Local Binaryen `version_130` probes showed HSO folds conditional external `throw` and `return_call` set values into `struct.new`, and also folds a conditional `return_call` inside active `try_table`; conditional `throw` inside an active catch remains a negative follow-up. Starshine previously retained `struct.set` for the three positives because `return_call*` / `throw*` were classified as skipped-local-set hazards and `return_call*` was treated as an active-catch escape. HSO now treats those terminators as function-external exits for skip-local-set purposes, keeps `unreachable` as a no-fold boundary, and removes `return_call*` from active-catch escape blocking while ordinary `call*` / `throw*` still block there. Focused HSO tests passed `81/81`, `moon info`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` `2709/2709`, full `moon test` `6022/6022`, native `src/cmd` build passed with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-external-exits-10000` compared `10000/10000` with `10000` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `0` command/validation/property/generator failures. Broader in-function branch/catch negatives remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix disappearing-bad-get control fold

- Filed [`raw/research/0796-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-disappearing-bad-get.md`](raw/research/0796-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-disappearing-bad-get.md). Binaryen `version_130` folds the exact `control-flow-in-set-value-sequence-yes` case where a branch-valued `struct.set` can move into the constructor because the only bad target-local read is inside the skipped owner tail and no outside read follows the dropped block. Starshine now tracks owner-external roots during nested HSO traversal and allows a branch-only root immediately before a matching later `struct.set` to move before the delayed fresh-struct `local.set` only when the target local is not touched after the owner expression. Added a red-first focused positive while the `0795` outside-read negative remains green. Focused HSO tests passed `78/78`, `moon fmt` passed, native `src/cmd` build passed with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-disappearing-bad-get-10000` compared `10000/10000` with `10000` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `0` command/validation/property/generator failures. Safe external throw/return-call coverage and broader in-function branch/catch negatives remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix nested control-sequence folds

- Filed [`raw/research/0795-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-nested-control-sequence.md`](raw/research/0795-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-nested-control-sequence.md). Binaryen `version_130` lit includes nested `drop(block(result ...))` control-flow sequence families; Starshine previously did not run HSO inside those nested blocks, so the safe first store was not folded. HSO now descends through ordinary expression children to reach nested control regions while preserving active-catch context for `try` / `try_table` regions. Added focused safe external-call coverage, a nested sequence that folds the first safe store while retaining an unsafe escaping branch-valued store, and a multi-branch escaping-local negative. Focused HSO tests passed `77/77`, `moon info`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` `2705/2705`, full `moon test` `6018/6018`, native `src/cmd` build passed with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-control-sequence-10000-rerun` compared `10000/10000` with `10000` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `0` command/validation/property/generator failures. The exact one-disappearing-bad-get exception remains open as a Starshine gap, not an accepted non-goal.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix in-function catch control folds

- Filed [`raw/research/0794-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-in-function-catch-control.md`](raw/research/0794-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-in-function-catch-control.md). Binaryen `version_130` lit keeps `struct.set` when a moved call value is inside a `try_table` caught by the same function, because folding would delay the fresh-struct local assignment until after a call that can throw to the in-function catch. The focused Starshine regression failed first by removing `struct.set`; HSO now threads active catch depth through region traversal and blocks moved call/throw/return-call values inside catchable `try`/`try_table` bodies while preserving the function-external `return` fold from `0793`. Focused HSO tests passed `74/74`, `moon fmt` passed, native `src/cmd` build passed with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-catch-negative-10000` compared `10000/10000` with `10000` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `0` command/validation/property/generator failures. Unsafe in-function branch negatives, broader safe external call/throw coverage, return-call classification, O4z slot/neighborhood evidence, and final closeout remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix function-return control folds

- Filed [`raw/research/0793-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-function-return-control.md`](raw/research/0793-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-function-return-control.md). Binaryen `version_130` lit/source and a local `wasm-opt --all-features --heap-store-optimization -S` probe show that a tee-wrapped `struct.set` value containing a plain `return` can fold into the constructor because the taken return exits the function and cannot skip a later in-function read. The focused Starshine regression failed first with retained `struct.set`; `hso_subtree_may_skip_local_set_from_root(...)` now treats plain `Return` as function-external while leaving catchable throws and other terminators conservative. Focused HSO tests passed `73/73`, `moon fmt` passed, native `src/cmd` build passed with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-function-return-10000` compared `10000/10000` with `10000` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `0` command/validation/property/generator failures. Broader in-function catch/branch negatives, return-call classification, O4z slot/neighborhood evidence, and final closeout remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Add unreachable boundary coverage

- Filed [`raw/research/0792-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-unreachable-boundaries.md`](raw/research/0792-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-unreachable-boundaries.md). Added coverage-only HSO tests for Binaryen `version_130`'s no-fold boundary around unreachable constructor operands and unreachable set-value operands, leaving cleanup to later DCE. Focused HSO tests passed `72/72`. The set-value fixture uses a block-wrapped unreachable value because the exact direct-root lit shape currently exposes a local HOT lowering fixture-surface limitation; this is recorded as local surface risk, not as an accepted HSO semantic non-goal. Generic DSE/load-forwarding wording, exact-cast local-surface limits, final-element swap coverage, O4z slot/neighborhood evidence, and final closeout remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix memory/table blocker swaps

- Filed [`raw/research/0791-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-memory-table-swap.md`](raw/research/0791-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-memory-table-swap.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that ordinary `i32.store` and `table.set` blockers are not hard HSO fold boundaries: `trySwap(...)` preserves those stores, moves a pure fresh-struct local assignment past them, and folds the later `struct.set` into the constructor. This supersedes the ordinary-memory-store boundary claim in `0790` while preserving the array-store and generic DSE/load-forwarding non-goal. Red-first focused tests failed with retained `struct.set`; after relaxing Starshine swap blockers for non-call/non-throw/non-control roots and preserving the constructor ping-pong guard, focused HSO tests passed `70/70`, `moon fmt` passed, native `src/cmd` build passed with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-memory-table-swap-10000` compared `10000/10000` with `10000` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `0` command/validation/property/generator failures.

Added coverage-only HSO boundary tests showing current Binaryen-scoped non-goals: `array.new_default` / `array.set` / `array.get` remains untouched, and an ordinary `i32.store` blocker keeps a later `struct.set` from folding. ## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Add core chain coverage

- Filed [`raw/research/0789-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-core-chain-coverage.md`](raw/research/0789-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-core-chain-coverage.md). Added coverage-only focused HSO tests for repeated same-field stores where the last value wins in the folded constructor, and wrong-target-local stores that must not fold into an unrelated fresh constructor. ## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix descriptor loop outer branches

- Filed [`raw/research/0788-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-loop-outer-branch.md`](raw/research/0788-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-loop-outer-branch.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that HSO folds a moved call into `struct.new_desc` when the descriptor operand is an outer result `block` containing a `loop` whose branch targets that outer block, while the existing self-branching descriptor-loop negative still blocks. Starshine overblocked the outer-branch case until descriptor loop-control barrier detection tracked the active loop label and treated branch-like nodes as loop-control barriers only when they target that active loop. Evidence: red-first focused test failed with retained `struct.set`, final HSO tests `65/65`, `moon fmt`, post-handoff native `src/cmd` build, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-desc-loop-outer-branch-10000` with `10000/10000` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `0` command/validation/property/generator failures. Broader descriptor casts/traps, later-field directional checks, target/control hazards, table/final-element swap coverage, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.
## [2026-06-20] passes/optimize-instructions | Add OI-I i31 upcast ref.eq proof

- Filed [`raw/research/0781-2026-06-20-optimize-instructions-oi-i-i31-upcast-ref-eq.md`](raw/research/0781-2026-06-20-optimize-instructions-oi-i-i31-upcast-ref-eq.md) for the twenty-fifth `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-I]` sub-slice. Starshine now folds same-local equality through immediate nullable `ref.cast(local.get)` operands when the local declared heap is absolute `i31` and the cast target is an already-modeled `i31` supertype, while keeping arbitrary cast skipping, descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT behavior, broader nullable-local flow facts, and `any`-typed `ref.eq` validation out of scope.
- Evidence: Binaryen oracle folded the i31 upcast equality probes to `i32.const 1`. Red-first `*i31 equality through nullable upcast ref.cast*` failed before implementation because `ref.eq` remained; final focused `*i31 equality through nullable upcast ref.cast*` passed `1/1`, `*ref*` passed `32/32`, and `*optimize-instructions*` passed `162/162`. Broader validation and direct compare are recorded in the research note.

## [2026-06-20] passes/global-struct-inference-desc-cast | Add exact-target desc-cast support

- Added exact-target representation for core `RefCast` / `RefCastDescEq` instructions plus binary encode/decode roundtrip coverage in [`src/binary/tests.mbt`](../../src/binary/tests.mbt), so Starshine no longer drops the exact cast-target bit needed by Binaryen `gsi-desc-cast` strict-subtype cases.
- Extended [`src/passes/global_struct_inference.mbt`](../../src/passes/global_struct_inference.mbt) and [`src/passes/global_struct_inference_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/global_struct_inference_test.mbt) so exact target casts can rewrite to exact `ref.cast_desc_eq` even when non-exact casts to the same target bail out due strict subtypes. Updated the GSI desc-cast docs and [`agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md) to keep only operand breadth and dedicated generator coverage open.
- Evidence: red-first focused GSI test first failed because `Instruction::ref_cast_type` did not exist, then focused GSI desc-cast tests passed `64/64`, focused binary tests passed `103/103`, `moon info` passed with existing warnings, `moon fmt` passed, and full `moon test` passed `6254/6254`.

## [2026-06-20] passes/global-struct-inference-desc-cast | Activate partial sibling implementation

- Starshine `global-struct-inference-desc-cast` is no longer boundary-only: [`src/passes/optimize.mbt`](../../src/passes/optimize.mbt) registers it as a module pass, [`src/passes/pass_manager.mbt`](../../src/passes/pass_manager.mbt) dispatches it through GSI-family desc-cast mode, and [`scripts/lib/pass-fuzz-compare-task.ts`](../../scripts/lib/pass-fuzz-compare-task.ts) maps it to Binaryen `--gsi-desc-cast`.
- Added focused tests in [`src/passes/global_struct_inference_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/global_struct_inference_test.mbt) for singleton descriptor-global and nullable positives plus closed-world-required, zero/multiple descriptor-global, and strict-subtype bailouts. Updated [`binaryen/passes/global-struct-inference-desc-cast/starshine-strategy.md`](binaryen/passes/global-struct-inference-desc-cast/starshine-strategy.md), [`index.md`](binaryen/passes/global-struct-inference-desc-cast/index.md), [`implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/global-struct-inference-desc-cast/implementation-structure-and-tests.md), [`fuzzing.md`](binaryen/passes/global-struct-inference-desc-cast/fuzzing.md), and [`agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md) to keep exact-target casts, operand breadth, and a dedicated closed-world descriptor-cast fuzz profile open.
- Evidence: `moon info`, `moon fmt`, focused GSI desc-cast tests `63/63`, `moon test src/passes` `2893/2893`, `moon test src/cmd` `164/164`, full `moon test` `6252/6252`, native `src/cmd` build, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-global-struct-inference-desc-cast-10000-rerun` with `7602/10000` compared, `7602` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `20` Binaryen/tool command failures.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix descriptor loop operands

- Filed [`raw/research/0787-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-loop.md`](raw/research/0787-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-loop.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that HSO folds a moved call into `struct.new_desc` when the descriptor operand is a branchless `loop` over an immutable descriptor global, but keeps the later `struct.set` when the descriptor loop branches to itself. Starshine overblocked the branchless loop until descriptor operand effect classification learned loop-body recursion with control clearing only for branchless/control-free loops. Evidence: red-first focused test failed with retained `struct.set`, final HSO tests `64/64`, `moon fmt`, and native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings. Per explicit user instruction, the ordinary 10000-case direct compare was skipped for this slice; a started compare command was aborted before summary and is not signoff evidence. Broader descriptor casts/outer branches/traps, later-field directional checks, target/control hazards, table/final-element swap coverage, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix descriptor block self-branch operands

- Filed [`raw/research/0786-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-block-branch.md`](raw/research/0786-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-block-branch.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that HSO folds a moved call into `struct.new_desc` when the descriptor operand is a `block` over an immutable descriptor global that branches to its own result label; Starshine overblocked this by treating the descriptor block's internal branch as an ordering barrier. Evidence: red-first focused test failed with retained `struct.set`, final HSO tests `62/62`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-desc-block-branch-10000` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures. Broader descriptor branches/casts/loops/traps, later-field directional checks, target/control hazards, table/final-element swap coverage, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix descriptor if operands

- Filed [`raw/research/0785-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-if.md`](raw/research/0785-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-if.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that HSO folds a moved call into `struct.new_desc` when the descriptor operand is a pure-condition `if` over immutable descriptor globals, but retains the later `struct.set` when the descriptor `if` condition calls. Starshine overblocked the pure descriptor-`if` case until descriptor operand effect classification learned `if` child/branch recursion with descriptor-aware effects. Evidence: red-first focused test failed with retained `struct.set`, final HSO tests `61/61`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-desc-if-10000` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures. Broader descriptor expressions, later-field directional checks, target/control hazards, table/final-element swap coverage, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix descriptor local/block operands

- Filed [`raw/research/0784-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-local-block.md`](raw/research/0784-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-local-block.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that HSO folds a moved call into `struct.new_desc` when the descriptor operand is a block-wrapped immutable descriptor `global.get` or a descriptor `local.get`; Starshine overblocked both until descriptor operand effect classification learned descriptor-local purity and descriptor-aware wrapper recursion. Evidence: red-first focused tests, final HSO tests `59/59`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-desc-local-block-10000-final` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures. Later-field directional checks, target/control hazards, table/final-element swap coverage, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Add swap memory and ping-pong coverage

- Filed [`raw/research/0783-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-swap-memory-and-pingpong.md`](raw/research/0783-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-swap-memory-and-pingpong.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that `trySwap(...)` folds a non-trapping `memory.size` constructor operand across an unrelated mutable `global.set`, keeps a potentially trapping `i32.load` constructor operand before an unrelated mutable `global.set`, and does not fold through an intervening constructor local-set ping-pong shape. Starshine already matched these behaviors, so this was a coverage-only slice: added focused HSO tests plus a memory+global helper fixture. Evidence: focused HSO tests `57/57`; no native rebuild or 10000-case direct compare was required because no implementation behavior changed. Broader table/final-element swap coverage, descriptor expressions, remaining later-field directional checks, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix immutable descriptor swap overblocking

- Filed [`raw/research/0782-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-immutable-descriptor-swap.md`](raw/research/0782-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-immutable-descriptor-swap.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that `trySwap(...)` folds a `struct.new_desc` whose constructor operand is an immutable descriptor `global.get` across an unrelated mutable `global.set`. The focused Starshine positive failed first because the `0781` constructor operand effect summary used raw HOT effects and overblocked immutable descriptor globals; `hso_struct_new_operand_effect_mask(...)` now uses module-context-aware descriptor operand effect classification for constructor children while preserving the mutable-global blocker. Evidence: focused HSO tests `54/54`, `moon test src/passes` `2892/2892`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, `moon info` with existing generator warnings, full `moon test` `6251/6251`, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-immutable-descriptor-swap-10000` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures. Broader swap legality, descriptor operands, remaining later-field directional checks, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix constructor/global swap directionality

- Filed [`raw/research/0781-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-swap-constructor-global.md`](raw/research/0781-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-swap-constructor-global.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that `trySwap(...)` keeps a constructor operand `global.get` before a later mutable `global.set`, while a `struct.new_default` constructor can still swap across an unrelated `global.set`. The focused Starshine negative failed first because swap legality only considered the skipped root and target-local hazards; `hso_struct_new_operand_effect_mask(...)` now feeds constructor operand effects into `hso_root_can_swap_before_local_struct_new(...)` before the swap. Evidence: focused HSO tests `53/53`, `moon test src/passes` `2891/2891`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, `moon info` with existing generator warnings, full `moon test` `6250/6250`, and direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-swap-constructor-global-10000` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures (`binaryen-rec-group-zero=22`, `binaryen-bad-section-size=1`). Broader swap legality, descriptor operands, remaining later-field directional checks, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix descriptor global/call movement

- Filed [`raw/research/0780-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-global-call.md`](raw/research/0780-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-global-call.md). Binaryen `version_130` probes showed that HSO folds a moved call into `struct.new_desc` when the descriptor operand is an immutable `global.get`, but keeps the later `struct.set` when that descriptor global is mutable. The focused Starshine positive failed first because broad effect masks treated descriptor `global.get` and moved calls as always ordered; `hso_desc_operand_effects_for_node(...)` now treats descriptor global reads as pure only when `HotModuleContext` proves the global immutable, and descriptor constructor shallow wrapper effects no longer add an extra barrier beyond the descriptor operand itself. Evidence: focused HSO tests `52/52`, `moon test src/passes` `2890/2890`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-descriptor-global-call-10000` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures (`binaryen-rec-group-zero=22`, `binaryen-bad-section-size=1`), `moon info` passed with existing generator warnings, and full `moon test` passed `6249/6249`. Broader descriptor operand expressions, remaining later-field directional checks, swap, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix directional trap/local movement

- Filed [`raw/research/0779-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-directional-trap-local.md`](raw/research/0779-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-directional-trap-local.md). A Binaryen `version_130` oracle probe showed that HSO folds a later local-only `struct.set` value before a trapping later constructor field (`i32.load`), because local state is not externally observable if the load traps. The focused Starshine test failed first because the old symmetric trap barrier kept `struct.set`; `hso_mask_ordered_before(...)` now models the movement check directionally and lets trap barriers block non-local writes without blocking local-only values. Evidence: focused HSO tests `50/50`, `moon test src/passes` `2888/2888`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-directional-trap-local-10000-rerun` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures (`binaryen-rec-group-zero=22`, `binaryen-bad-section-size=1`), `moon info` passed with existing generator warnings, and full `moon test` passed `6247/6247`. Descriptor, remaining later-field directional, swap, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/heap-store-optimization | Fix plain-constructor shallow-effect overblocking

- Filed [`raw/research/0778-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-shallow-effects-fold.md`](raw/research/0778-2026-06-20-heap-store-optimization-shallow-effects-fold.md). Added focused HSO tests for old-field call preservation, later-field call barriers, and the Binaryen lit/source family where a moved call folds into a plain `struct.new` while preserving the old field call. The moved-call test failed before the implementation because Starshine modeled every `struct.new*` wrapper as memory/trap-effecting; `hso_struct_new_shallow_effects(...)` now treats plain `struct.new` / `struct.new_default` wrappers as effect-free and keeps descriptor constructors conservative. Evidence: focused HSO tests `49/49`, `moon test src/passes` `2887/2887`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build with existing warnings, direct 10000-case compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap-store-optimization-shallow-effects-10000` compared `9977/10000`, normalized `9977`, had `0` mismatches, and had `23` Binaryen/oracle command failures (`binaryen-rec-group-zero=22`, `binaryen-bad-section-size=1`), `moon info` passed with existing generator warnings, and full `moon test` passed `6246/6246`. Descriptor, later-field directional, swap, and O4z slot/neighborhood evidence remain open.

## [2026-06-20] passes/optimize-instructions | Add OI-I impossible struct/array ref.test/ref.cast proof

- Filed [`raw/research/0774-2026-06-20-optimize-instructions-oi-i-impossible-struct-array-test-cast.md`](raw/research/0774-2026-06-20-optimize-instructions-oi-i-impossible-struct-array-test-cast.md) for the eighteenth `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-I]` sub-slice. Starshine now folds `ref.test` to `i32.const 0` and rewrites matching `ref.cast` to `unreachable` when a declared non-null absolute `struct` local targets absolute `array`, or a declared non-null absolute `array` local targets absolute `struct`.

## [2026-06-19] passes/optimize-instructions | Add OI-G constant store value slice

- Filed [`raw/research/0747-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-g-const-store-value.md`](raw/research/0747-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-g-const-store-value.md) for the sixteenth `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-G]` sub-slice. The slice adds Binaryen-style `optimizeStoredValue` constant truncation before narrow stores: `i32.store8`, `i32.store16`, `i64.store8`, `i64.store16`, and `i64.store32` now replace oversized constants with the low bits observed by the store.
- Evidence: local Binaryen oracle probe folded representative constants (`-1` to `255` for `i32.store8`, `65537` to `1` for `i32.store16`, and `4294967296` to `0` for `i64.store32`). Red-first focused `moon test --target native src/passes/optimize_instructions_test.mbt --filter '*constant narrow store values*'` failed the new positive before implementation. After implementation, the same filter passed `1/1`, `*narrow stores*` passed `5/5`, `*memory*` passed `21/21`, focused `*optimize-instructions*` passed `128/128`, `moon fmt` passed, `moon test src/passes` passed `2640/2640`, native `src/cmd` release build passed with existing warnings, and `moon info` passed with existing warnings. Direct compare initially timed out and then hit a no-return-code GenValid batch failure before a clean rerun wrote `.tmp/pass-fuzz-optimize-instructions-oi-g-const-store-value-10000`, compared `54/10000`, with `27` normalized matches, `27` raw mismatches, `0` validation/property/generator failures, and `1` Binaryen/tool command failure (`binaryen-rec-group-zero`); cache wasm-smith `28` hits / `0` misses, Binaryen `54` hits / `0` misses, Binaryen failures `1` hit / `0` misses. Agent classification: remaining raw mismatches are known Starshine-win constant-if and redundant-sign-extension output-shape families, not new narrow-store semantic failures; failure artifacts contained no `store8`, `store16`, or `store32` occurrences. Updated [`binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md`](binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md), [`binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/starshine-hot-ir-strategy.md`](binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/starshine-hot-ir-strategy.md), [`index.md`](index.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md); `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-G]` remains active for broader load-store/gate work.

## [2026-06-19] passes/optimize-instructions | Add OI-G load-call offset boundary

- Filed [`raw/research/0745-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-g-load-call-offset-boundary.md`](raw/research/0745-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-g-load-call-offset-boundary.md) for the fourteenth `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-G]` sub-slice. The slice records an explicit fail-closed decision for `load-call-optimize-instructions-noop`: Starshine's public pipeline keeps mixed plain-load plus call functions unchanged even when the direct HOT pass can fold a constant-pointer static memarg offset. This keeps the raw gate as a release performance/representation boundary until a broader source-backed escape proof exists.
- Evidence: focused `moon test --target native src/passes/optimize_instructions_test.mbt --filter '*load-call*'` passed `1/1`; the public-pipeline fixture traces `pass[optimize-instructions]:skip-raw reason=load-call-optimize-instructions-noop`, keeps `i32.const I32(8)` plus `offset=U64(4)`, and does not materialize `i32.const I32(12)`. Focused `*skips*` passed `9/9`, focused `*optimize-instructions*` passed `126/126`, `moon fmt` passed, `moon test src/passes` passed `2638/2638`, native `src/cmd` release build passed with no work needed, and `moon info` passed with existing warnings. A Binaryen oracle probe with `wasm-opt .tmp/oi-g-load-call-offset-boundary.wat --optimize-instructions -S --print | grep -E 'i32\\.const|i32\\.load|call'` folded the same fixture to `i32.const 12` before the call, so this is a documented Starshine raw-gate boundary rather than a parity claim. Direct `--pass optimize-instructions --count 10000 --seed 0x5eed --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` wrote `.tmp/pass-fuzz-optimize-instructions-oi-g-load-call-offset-boundary-10000`, compared `56/10000`, with `28` normalized matches, `28` raw mismatches, `0` validation/property/generator failures, and `1` Binaryen/tool command failure (`binaryen-rec-group-zero`); cache wasm-smith `29` hits / `0` misses, Binaryen `56` hits / `0` misses, Binaryen failures `1` hit / `0` misses. Agent classification: remaining raw mismatches are known Starshine-win constant-if and redundant-sign-extension output-shape families, not new load-call/offset semantic failures; failure artifacts contained no `load-call`, `i32.load offset`, `i64.load offset`, `i32.store offset`, or `i64.store offset` occurrences. Updated [`binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md`](binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md), [`binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/starshine-hot-ir-strategy.md`](binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/starshine-hot-ir-strategy.md), [`index.md`](index.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md); `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-G]` remains active for broader load-store canonicalization and any future source-backed raw-gate escape.

## [2026-06-19] passes/optimize-instructions | Add OI-G memory64 constant offset slice

- Filed [`raw/research/0744-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-g-memory64-const-offset.md`](raw/research/0744-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-g-memory64-const-offset.md) for the thirteenth `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-G]` sub-slice. The slice extends Binaryen `OptimizeInstructions.cpp::optimizeMemoryAccess(...)` constant-pointer static-offset folding to memory64 scalar loads/stores: constant `i64` address plus nonzero memarg offset is folded into the address and the memarg offset is reset to zero only when unsigned `u64(address) + offset` does not wrap. Direct `--pass optimize-instructions --count 10000 --seed 0x5eed --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` wrote `.tmp/pass-fuzz-optimize-instructions-oi-g-memory64-const-offset-10000`, compared `54/10000`, with `27` normalized matches, `27` raw mismatches, `0` validation/property/generator failures, and `1` Binaryen/tool command failure (`binaryen-rec-group-zero`). Agent classification: remaining raw mismatches are known Starshine-win constant-if and redundant-sign-extension output-shape families, not new memory64 constant-offset semantic failures; failure artifacts contained no `memory64`, `i64.load offset`, `i64.store offset`, `i32.load offset`, or `i32.store offset` occurrences. Updated [`binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md`](binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md), [`binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/starshine-hot-ir-strategy.md`](binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/starshine-hot-ir-strategy.md), [`index.md`](index.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md); `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-G]` remains active for broader load-store/gate work.

## [2026-06-19] passes/optimize-instructions | Complete OI-C raw gate audit

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-C]` by filing [`raw/research/0728-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-c-raw-gates.md`](raw/research/0728-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-c-raw-gates.md). The audit added public-pipeline trace assertions for every OI raw no-op gate family and a positive fixture proving neutral-add cleanup still runs when no raw gate applies.
- Fixed a trace-accountability bug in [`../../src/passes/pass_manager.mbt`](../../src/passes/pass_manager.mbt): the structured and loop-specific OI performance guards now run before the broad call/local-write guard, so `structured-call-branch-optimize-instructions-noop`, `block-return-call-branch-optimize-instructions-noop`, and `loop-branch-local-write-optimize-instructions-noop` are reachable public reasons. Evidence: red-first focused structured test initially reported `call-local-write-optimize-instructions-noop`; after the fix, `moon test --target native src/passes/optimize_instructions_test.mbt --filter '*optimize-instructions skips*'` passed `9/9`, the outside-gate public cleanup filter passed `1/1`, `moon fmt` passed, `moon test src/passes` passed `2600/2600`, native `src/cmd` build passed, and a direct 10-case smoke completed with `5` normalized matches and `5` raw mismatches classified as the known OI-B scalar/default parity-gap family for `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-D]`. A 100-case smoke timed out before `result.json`. Updated [`binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md`](binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/index.md), [`index.md`](index.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md); `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-D]` is next.

The post-RUB-P final direct lane `.tmp/pass-fuzz-remove-unused-brs-rub-o-post-rub-p-final-100000` compared `99751/100000`, with `57148` normalized matches, `42601` cleanup-normalized matches, `2` raw mismatches, and `249` Binaryen/tool command failures (`219` rec-group-zero, `12` bad-section-size, `11` command-failed, `6` table-index-out-of-range, `1` invalid-tag-index).
- The remaining raw mismatches are agent-classified Starshine wins, not semantic gaps: `case-027803-wasm-smith` removes a pure SIMD dropped expression before unconditional `unreachable` (Binaryen 69 bytes, Starshine 61 bytes), and `case-048903-wasm-smith` removes pure `memory.size` plus `f64.abs` drops before unconditional `br` (Binaryen 68 bytes, Starshine 54 bytes). ## [2026-06-19] passes/remove-unused-brs | Complete late switch one-target collapse

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-P]` for the local HOT subset of Binaryen `FinalOptimizer::visitSwitch(...)`. [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt) now collapses lifted value-carrying one-target `br_table`s to `drop(selector); br payload` when the selector is const/local.get and payload values are locally reorder-safe, matching the Binaryen requirement that the selector can be moved before the value. Effectful selector/value pairs and child-less stack-payload switches stay conservative.
- Added focused coverage in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt) for the local-selector positive and an effectful call/call reorder negative. Evidence: focused RUB tests passed, `moon test src/passes` and full `moon test` passed, native `src/cmd` release build passed, and direct compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-remove-unused-brs-rub-p-10000` compared `9977/10000` with `0` mismatches and `23` Binaryen/tool command failures (`22` rec-group-zero, `1` bad-section-size). Updated [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/starshine-strategy.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/starshine-strategy.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md). `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-O]` final closeout remains open until a fresh post-RUB-P final signoff run.

## [2026-06-19] passes/remove-unused-brs | Complete selectify cost parity

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-K]` for direct value-`if` `selectify` behavior. [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt) now applies Binaryen-shaped unconditionalization cost thresholds to direct HOT selectify candidates: speed mode preserves costly integer div/rem and trapping int-convert arms, balanced shrink mode permits a one-costly-arm shape, and full shrink mode can selectify for size. Existing scalar select-emission, reorder/effect, and `local.tee` read-safety gates remain in force.
- Added focused coverage in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt) for preserving a costly `i32.rem_s` value-if at speed shrink level and selectifying the same shape under `shrink_level=1`. Refreshed [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/select-and-condition-rewrites.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/select-and-condition-rewrites.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/starshine-strategy.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/starshine-strategy.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md). Branch hints and `remove-unused-brs-never-unconditionalize` remain the documented `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` unsupported boundary; `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-O]` final signoff remains open.

## [2026-06-19] passes/remove-unused-brs | Document branch-hint and never-unconditionalize boundary

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` as a source/docs boundary slice. [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/select-and-condition-rewrites.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/select-and-condition-rewrites.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md) now record that Starshine has no expression-level `metadata.code.branch_hint` representation, parser/lowerer path, binary code-metadata section, or RUB remap target. Binaryen `BranchHints::copyTo`, `copyFlippedTo`, `applyAndTo`, `applyOrTo`, `flip`, and `clear` therefore remain unsupported metadata preservation until a dedicated code-metadata implementation lands.
- The same boundary covers Binaryen `remove-unused-brs-never-unconditionalize`: Starshine currently has no `--pass-arg` / pass-option equivalent, so condition-combining, selectifying, and restructuring RUB rewrites are ordinary direct-pass behavior rather than configurable never-unconditionalize parity. Reopening requires a code-metadata representation plus parser/lowerer/binary or opaque-section policy, branch-hint remap tests, and explicit pass-option tests for the guarded rewrites. `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-K]` selectify remains open, so `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-O]` final closeout is not claimed.

## [2026-06-19] passes/remove-unused-brs | Complete optimizeSetIf local-store parity

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-L]` for the locally representable Binaryen `FinalOptimizer::optimizeSetIf(...)` family. [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt) now rewrites `local.set` and `local.tee` of one-result `if`s when either arm is a simple branch or a same-local copy arm. Flipped arms build `i32.eqz` conditions; tee copy arms preserve the result with a block containing a one-armed setter and trailing `local.get`; branch-arm extraction preserves surviving `local.tee` results and then re-enters the region pass so exposed nested set-if copy arms clean up recursively.
- Added focused coverage in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt) for then-arm copy flipping, tee copy-arm result blocks, else-arm branch extraction, and recursive cleanup after branch extraction. Refreshed [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/branch-exit-and-payload-rewrites.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/branch-exit-and-payload-rewrites.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md). `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-K]` selectify and `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` branch-hints / `never-unconditionalize` remain open.

## [2026-06-19] passes/remove-unused-brs | Add restructureIf self-branch parity

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-J]` for the locally representable Binaryen `FinalOptimizer::restructureIf(...)` family. [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt) now rewrites named blocks whose leading self-target `br_if` has no other target uses: void forms become an outer one-arm `if` with an `i32.eqz` condition, dropped value forms become a result `if` when the value is locally reorder-safe, and side-effectful value forms become `select` when the remaining block arm is pure enough to execute unconditionally.
- Added focused coverage in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt) for void `br_if` prefix restructuring, dropped value result-`if` restructuring, side-effectful value-to-`select`, additional-target-use preservation, and effectful-fallthrough preservation. Updated prior one-arm payload branch expectations for the new late restructure step, plus [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md). Branch-hint metadata and `never-unconditionalize` remain explicit `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` boundaries.

## [2026-06-19] passes/remove-unused-brs | Add tablify dense ladder parity

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-I]` for Binaryen `FinalOptimizer::tablify(...)` no-payload equality ladders. [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt) now recognizes dense `br_if` runs using `i32.eq` or `i32.eqz`, shares a first `local.tee` selector through later `local.get`s, permits distinct branch targets, rejects duplicate constants, applies Binaryen's `MIN_NUM=3` / `MAX_RANGE=1024` / `range <= arms * 3` thresholds, and emits a wrapper-default `br_table` with min-offset selector subtraction.
- Added focused coverage in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt) for distinct-target dense ladders, `eqz` zero cases, duplicate-constant preservation, and sparse-range preservation. - Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-H]` for the shrink-mode no-payload adjacent `br_if` merge family in Binaryen `FinalOptimizer::visitBlock`. [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt) now merges adjacent same-target `br_if` roots by building an `i32.or` condition when the later condition is locally safe to speculate, for both child-form and lifted stack-form branch conditions.

## [2026-06-18] passes/remove-unused-brs | Add EH caught-throw cleanup

- Completed `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-E]` for the Binaryen `version_130` `visitThrow(...)` safe `try_table` family. [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs.mbt) now rewrites definitely caught exact-tag `throw` roots to payload-carrying `br` roots and rewrites non-ref `catch_all` throws to payload-preserving `drop` roots followed by `br`. The matcher walks the local catcher stack from innermost outward and remains conservative for tag mismatches, `catch_ref`, `catch_all_ref`, earlier catch-ref-before-catch-all exnref transport, and HOT `Try` mixed-control boundaries.
- Added red-first focused coverage in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_brs_test.mbt) for exact catches, `catch_all` payload dropping, tag mismatch, `catch_ref`, `catch_all_ref`, and catch-ref-before-catch-all negatives. [`../../src/passes/pass_manager.mbt`](../../src/passes/pass_manager.mbt) now lets the raw candidate gate lift `try_table` bodies containing `throw` without widening unrelated O4z raw skip exceptions or broad nested RUB traversal.
- Updated [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/parity.md), [`binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/visit-order-and-bailouts.md`](binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/visit-order-and-bailouts.md), and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md). Remaining RUB audit slices stay open for `sinkBlocks`, GC BrOn cleanup, general JumpThreader, branch hints / `never-unconditionalize`, and late final-optimizer families; legacy old-`try` mixed-control is recorded as a local public-pipeline representation blocker.

- Reconciled the prior `.tmp/self-ssa-nomerge-loop-carrier-fix2-20260615` `defined=32 abs=59` tuple: targeted current extraction shows it is stale as an actionable semantic issue and is masked-local-equal with equal encoded body size, but local-count-losing for Starshine (`9` body i32 locals versus Binaryen `7`) rather than a measured win. - Updated [`binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/parity.md) and [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md). No executable pass behavior changed; `bun test scripts/lib/self-opt-task.test.ts` passed `15/15` for the uncommitted diagnostic batching change.

## [2026-06-15] passes/ssa-nomerge | Bound loop-carried canonical-write scans

- Diagnosed the post-`abs59` debug-WASI self-compare stall as Starshine-side `ssa-nomerge` analysis work, not Binaryen. Direct `--ssa-nomerge` on `tests/node/dist/starshine-debug-wasi.wasm` timed out after `30s`, and tracing isolated the extracted repro to `--extract-functions=474 --ssa-nomerge` before `ssa_nomerge_build_rewrite_plan(...)` returned.
- Fixed the performance-toxic loop-carried canonical-write scan by adding visited sets to HOT node/region traversal and by collecting loop-carried locals before mapping them back to preserved write nodes. Also replaced the raw lowered loop-carried merge-local detector's per-local recursive scans with bounded read/write bitset collection.
- Evidence: `moon fmt`; focused `ssa_nomerge_test.mbt` passed `448/448`; native `moon build --target native --release src/cmd` completed with pre-existing unused-function warnings; direct debug-WASI `target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe --ssa-nomerge --out .tmp/diagnose-ssanm.wasm tests/node/dist/starshine-debug-wasi.wasm` now exits `0` in about `4s` instead of timing out at `30s`. The full self-compare still needs another performance pass: after this fix it produces Starshine/Binaryen artifacts, but a timed sample shows it is now spending time in downstream first-diff localization via repeated `cmd.exe --print-func`, not in the original empty-dir Starshine pass stall.

- Finding: Starshine's basic direct-call full-inlining thresholds mostly match Binaryen defaults, while differences cluster in repair/scheduler surfaces: broader `try_table` conservatism, no partial-inlining splitter, partial tail-call/name/nondefaultable-local repair, hand-expanded optimizing suffix, large-module suffix guard, and Starshine-only unreachable-cycle prediction/trim scaffolding.

Docs-only; no tests run.

## [2026-06-14] passes/ssa-nomerge | Refresh current debug-WASI replay and defer O4z decision

- Recorded explicit user direction to defer `[SSANM-010c]` until SSANM is otherwise fully implemented. The current `o4z-ssa-nomerge-noop` guard stays in place; no scheduling policy was chosen or applied.
- Completed classification-only `[SSANM-009b113]` through `[SSANM-009b115]` in [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md): fresh current-HEAD replay after `[SSANM-009b30a]` and `[SSANM-010b1]` first-diffs at `defined=1451 abs=1478`; targeted `abs=1479`, `1481`, `1483`, `1485`, `1489`, `1491`, and `1493` match; and non-exact candidates through `abs=1494` classify as representation-only local-count wins or neutral same-typed drift.
- Evidence: `moon build --target native --release src/cmd` reported `no work to do`; `bun scripts/self-optimize-compare.ts tests/node/dist/starshine-debug-wasi.wasm --out-dir .tmp/self-ssa-nomerge-current-head-20260614 --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe --ssa-nomerge` validated both outputs, reported Starshine size `3,149,379` bytes versus Binaryen `3,155,990`, pass-local time `0.195ms` versus `439.084ms`, and first diff `defined=1451 abs=1478`.
- Classification: `abs=1478` differs by a loop-carrier shape. Binaryen routes the next loop pair through a block-result carrier; Starshine writes the same next values directly into the loop-carried locals immediately before the same backedge. Because those local updates are adjacent to the branch with no intervening uses/effects, and because Starshine has a measured local/body-size win (`174` byte body with `15` body i32 locals versus Binaryen `182` bytes with `16` body i32 locals), this is a semantic-safe Starshine win, not an implementation gap. `abs=1480`, `1482`, `1484`, `1486`, `1487`, `1488`, `1492`, and `1494` have identical masked body tokens, equal encoded function bodies, and fewer Starshine body locals; `abs=1490` is equal-body/equal-local-count same-typed local-slot drift.
- Updated [`binaryen/no-dwarf-default-optimize-path.md`](binaryen/no-dwarf-default-optimize-path.md), [`binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/parity.md), and [`binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/implementation-structure-and-tests.md).

## [2026-06-14] passes/remove-unused-names | Fix SSANM early-neighborhood typed loop demotion

- Completed `[SSANM-010b1]` in [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md) by minimizing the `[SSANM-010b]` `--ssa-nomerge --dead-code-elimination --remove-unused-names` stack-underflow blocker to `remove-unused-names`, not `ssa-nomerge` mutation.
- Root cause: the extracted `Func 25` / original debug-WASI `Func 254` had a no-continue TypeIdx loop with an entry-param type. Raw/HOT `remove-unused-names` demoted TypeIdx loops to blocks, which can place loop entry operands inside the replacement block and underflow the block entry stack. The pass now preserves TypeIdx / entry-param loops while still rewriting nested bodies.
- Added red-first regression `remove-unused-names preserves dead typed loop with entry params` in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_names_test.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_names_test.mbt); implemented the raw guard in [`../../src/passes/pass_manager.mbt`](../../src/passes/pass_manager.mbt) and the HOT guard in [`../../src/passes/remove_unused_names.mbt`](../../src/passes/remove_unused_names.mbt).
- Evidence: focused `remove_unused_names_test.mbt` passed `26/26`; `moon fmt`; `moon test src/passes` passed `2472/2472`; native `moon build --target native --release src/cmd` completed with pre-existing pass-manager unused-function warnings. Rebuilt-native direct replay of `--remove-unused-names` on `.tmp/self-ssa-nomerge-o4z-early-neighborhood-20260614/func254-before.wasm` exited `0`, and `wasm-tools validate --features all .tmp/ssanm010b1-replay/func254-after.wasm` passed. The rebuilt-native three-pass prefix `--ssa-nomerge --dead-code-elimination --remove-unused-names tests/node/dist/starshine-debug-wasi.wasm` now exits `0` in about `5s` and writes `7,482,266` bytes.
- Updated [`binaryen/no-dwarf-default-optimize-path.md`](binaryen/no-dwarf-default-optimize-path.md), [`binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/parity.md`](binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/parity.md), and [`binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`](binaryen/passes/ssa-nomerge/implementation-structure-and-tests.md). `[SSANM-010c]` remains the explicit user-approved scheduling decision and must not be decided by this fix.

## [2026-06-14] passes/ssa-nomerge | Refresh O4z scheduling anchors

- Completed `[SSANM-010a]` in [`../../agent-todo.md`](../../agent-todo.md) as a source/docs refresh before the top-level O4z scheduling decision.
